HARD GUARDRAILS (violating any = failure):
- EDIT ONLY the champion repo /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent (branch gate-s0-s5). NEVER edit /home/polaris/wt/flywheel (read-only reference).
- FAITHFULNESS FROZEN: never modify src/polaris_graph/generator/provenance_generator.py or any strict_verify / citation-verification logic. READ ONLY. No new verification pass.
- Every new behavior behind a default-OFF flag (or a None-default kwarg) so the OFF path is BYTE-IDENTICAL to today's champion. Follow the repo's existing flag pattern.
- Additive & minimal. Prefer NEW modules over rewriting. Never invent a hard constraint that isn't from explicit prompt text (design rule).
- Scope must reach RETRIEVAL query generation — NEVER a post-fetch filter of a frozen corpus (the 997->131 anti-pattern).
- Do NOT run any full ~35-min live compose or RACE/FACT scoring (a workflow shell caps at 10 min). Use fast unit tests, OFFLINE compiles, BANKED replays, and query-gen TELEMETRY (no fetch) only. If a step needs a live run, BUILD + unit-test it and leave a documented harness; do not execute it.

SPEC DOCS (read both FIRST, in full):
- Consolidated design + build sequence: /home/polaris/polaris_project/GATE_DESIGN_CONSOLIDATED.md
- Sol's detailed hook map + schema: /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/reviews/gate_gate_design.md

IMPLEMENT S3 — Outline FEED + gap-scope fix (BANKED replay only).
- Refactor src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_agent.py:run_outline_agent_or_legacy into refine_outline_from_seed(seed_outline, contract, retrieval_projection, coverage_matrix, ...) with legacy path: seed = supplied_seed or legacy_call_outline(...). Preserve the legacy adapter (OFF path byte-identical).
- FEED: thread deliverable_spec/scope_spec from the contract; pre-load required_coverage/must_address as PENDING gaps in the gap ledger. Required topics are coverage obligations, NOT automatically headings (do not repeat the round1 coverage-to-heading bug). Explicit headings = exact title+order lock via existing apply_revision_ops(required_titles=...).

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

1	# The Research Planning Gate — Consolidated Design (Opus)
2	
3	**Consolidates two independent designs** (Sol / GPT-5.6 code-grounded, Fable 5) + a 2026 best-practices digest.
4	**Date:** 2026-07-16 · **Status:** DESIGN — build only after operator approval; build runs in a workflow, main session monitors.
5	**Target repo:** `/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent` (champion `bot/outline-agent-box @ df4118a`). Never edit `flywheel/`.
6	
7	> **The thesis both designers reached independently:** the gate produces ONE durable, typed **research contract**, then COMPILES it into levers the champion pipeline *already exposes but the driver never populates* — retrieval-backend routing (`ResearchFrame`), FS-Researcher query text, and `deliverable_spec`/`scope_spec`. **Scope always ADDS/ROUTES queries at retrieval; it NEVER filters a frozen corpus.** A disclosure-only compliance audit reads the same contract at the end. Faithfulness (`strict_verify`) is untouched.
8	
9	---
10	
11	## 1. Non-negotiables (carried from operator rulings)
12	1. **FAITHFULNESS FROZEN.** No edit to `src/polaris_graph/generator/provenance_generator.py` / `strict_verify` / the drop rule. No new verification pass. A conformance test asserts a clean diff and that every recompose path still traverses the unchanged verifier.
13	2. **No starvation.** Scope reaches FS-Researcher *query generation* and backend routing BEFORE the first fetch. Any post-fetch filter is a secondary safety net for explicit prohibitions only — never the sole scope mechanism. (The 997→131 filter that tanked RACE 0.4447→0.3264 is the banned anti-pattern.)
14	3. **Never invent a constraint.** Enforced by TYPE: a hard/binding term requires `origin == explicit` (a real prompt span) or an affirmative user action. Inferred/default terms may only weight, prefer, or route — never hard-gate, exclude, or become a stated requirement.
15	4. **Priority COVERAGE > INSIGHT > READABILITY.** Length is planning context, never a truncation gate. Required coverage drives the gap ledger. Tone is prose-only.
16	5. **Fail-open.** Any gate error → empty/degraded contract → every lever reads its current default → byte-identical to today's champion. The gate can only ADD guardrails; it can never abort a run or drop a source.
17	6. **Reuse, don't rebuild.** Reuse the validated rule-reader (`round1-if-compiler:constraint_extractor.py`), the intake extractors, FS-Researcher, the ResearchFrame router, `deliverable_spec`/`scope_spec`, the outline agent. New code is small and additive.
18	
19	---
20	
21	## 2. Architecture (both modes)
22	
23	```
24	caller passes mode EXPLICITLY (interactive | autonomous)   ← Sol: never infer mode from terminal/timeout
25	        │   benchmark driver ALWAYS passes autonomous
26	        ▼
27	[1 UNDERSTAND]  deterministic candidate extraction (rule-reader + intake extractors, exact spans)
28	        ▼
29	[2 COMPILE]     one structured LLM call → typed ResearchContract (every clause accounted for;
30	                each term tagged explicit/inferred/user + force hard/prefer/open)
31	        ▼
32	[3 VALIDATE+TRIAGE]  deterministic: span/quote equality, no inferred-hard, clause coverage, conflicts
33	        │                                    │
34	   INTERACTIVE                          AUTONOMOUS  (benchmark)
35	   show editable plan + diff            ask nobody, wait for nobody
36	   ask 0–3 MATERIAL questions           least-restrictive open defaults
37	   edit / answer / approve  ──┐         disclose assumptions; auto-pin      ← the two modes differ at ONLY this node
38	        ▼                     └──────────────┬─────────────┘
39	[4 PLAN+PIN]    2nd structured call → threads, query intents, outline seed, coverage matrix; hash+pin (immutable)
40	        ▼
41	[5 PROJECTIONS] same pinned artifact → retrieval | outline | compose | render   (typed per-stage views)
42	        ▼
43	[6 COMPLIANCE AUDIT]  term-level SATISFIED/FAILED/UNSATISFIABLE/UNKNOWN, routed to owning stage
44	                      (disclosure-only; strict_verify untouched)
45	```
46	
47	- **Interactive** (real users): ≤3 material questions; editable plan (Gemini-style natural-language edits); retrieval waits for approve/edit/answer/"use best judgment"; never crosses into autonomous on a timeout.
48	- **Autonomous** (benchmark; `PG_GATE_AUTONOMOUS=1`, the default in `scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py`): a pure `contract→contract` function, **no I/O, never blocks**. Every would-ask becomes an open-ended assumption surfaced in report front-matter. *Enforced by a test asserting zero `needs_input` exits with no input channel.* This is the load-bearing mode — without it the gate can't be RACE-scored.
49	
50	---
51	
52	## 3. The contract schema (merged)
53	Adopt **Sol's typed governance skeleton** (it's the more complete one) with **Fable's `Tagged`-provenance discipline** on every term:
54	
55	- **Envelope:** immutable `PlanningGateArtifact` — `mode`, `state`, `original_prompt`, `contract`, `plan`, `clarification_questions`, `revisions` (JSON-Patch), `contract_sha256`/`plan_sha256`/`artifact_sha256`. Reuse the existing `research_planner.py:serialize_plan_canonical`/`plan_sha256`.
56	- **Every term = `ContractTerm{value, origin(explicit|user|inferred|policy_default), force(hard|prefer|open), spans[], rationale, enforcement_stages[]}`.** `explicit` requires exact `prompt[start:end]` quote equality. **Hard force is invalid unless origin==explicit/user** (the mechanical no-invention guarantee).
57	- **Groups:** `ObjectiveSpec` (question, purpose, audience, output_language, depth), `ScopeSpec` (source_types, quality, languages, date, geography/jurisdiction, domains, named_sources, prohibited), `ContentSpec` (required/optional `CoverageRequirement`s incl. comparisons/metrics/counterevidence + entities + breadth/depth), `DeliverableSpec` (kind, sections w/ exact-title & order locks, length, visuals, citation, rhetoric/tone), plus `ambiguities[]`, `assumptions[]`, `conflicts[]`, `complexity`.
58	- **Plan (separate, adapts to evidence while contract stays pinned):** `threads`, `evidence_needs`, `query_intents` (each carrying its scope term-ids + language/date/source lanes), `outline_seed`, **`coverage_matrix`** (every binding term → owning stage(s) + ≥1 retrieval lane), `budget` (`mandatory_lane_count`, overflow = expand-or-fail, **never silently truncate a mandatory lane**), `stop_conditions` (semantic sufficiency, not a source-count quota).
59	
60	---
61	
62	## 4. The understanding engine
63	A **bounded hybrid**, not one blind call (both agree; digest confirms):
64	1. **Deterministic candidate pass** — reuse `round1-if-compiler` `constraint_extractor` ⊕ champion `intake_constraint_extractor` (`extract_constraints_regex`, `extract_instruction_slots`, `extract_scope_constraints`). Preserve exact offsets. **Deterministic wins on overlap; LLM fills gaps.** One candidate adapter — one canonical contract, no competing truth sources.
65	2. **Contract-compiler call** — structured JSON; system prompt forbids marking implications as explicit, forbids inventing date/geo/source/length, requires open/null for unspecified, decomposes compound prompts preserving dependencies, records ambiguities + assumptions + conflicts, returns `clause_coverage` (every operative clause dispositioned). **Raise the current 2,000-token `plan_research` cap** — inadequate for compound contracts (Sol); use provider-resolved max + bounded 2-call structure.
66	3. **Deterministic validate + triage** — quote equality, no inferred-hard, every candidate represented-or-rejected, every clause dispositioned, conflicts surfaced. One correction retry → else conservative span-verified fallback (raw prompt as objective, explicit rules only, open elsewhere; `compiler_degraded=true`).
67	4. **Plan-compiler call** — from the *validated contract*: smallest complete thread set (scale to complexity, no fixed quota), every evidence-bearing requirement gets ≥1 mandatory query intent, scope encoded in query intents (hard→query+backend filter, soft→ranking), coverage matrix built, exact headings only from explicit instruction.
68	
69	Cost: 2 small-model calls (glm-5.2, same as FS query-gen) — negligible vs retrieval+outline.
70	
71	---
72	
73	## 5. Per-stage enforcement (merged hook map)
74	
75	| Stage | What the contract does | Champion hook (verified) |
76	|---|---|---|
77	| **Gate entry** | compile/load artifact, require approved/auto_pinned, persist `planning_gate_artifact.json` | new `planning/research_planning_gate.py:run_research_planning_gate`; in `run_honest_sweep_r3.py:run_one_query` replace the raw `plan_research(_clean_question,…)` decision |
78	| **Retrieval (the no-starvation core)** | `source_types→evidence_needs` routes scholarly backends (S2/OpenAlex) = **GO FIND journals**; scope terms + languages + entities into query TEXT; hard scope → backend-native filter, soft → ranking | `live_retriever.py:run_live_retrieval` (accepts `research_frame`/`protocol`/`amplified_queries`); `fs_researcher_query_gen.py:_plan_expert_facet_queries` + `_multilingual_native_reserve` + `sub_entity_query_expander`; `expert_facet_planner._question_anchor`. **FS branch in `run_one_query` currently passes only `_clean_question` and discards `_research_plan.sub_queries` — fix that.** |
79	| **Outline (FEED)** | seed required sections + pre-load `required_coverage`/`must_address` as PENDING gaps so the deep-think loop CLOSES contract gaps; thread scope into gap search | `outline_agent.py:run_outline_agent_or_legacy` (already takes `deliverable_spec`/`scope_spec` → `outline_digest.build_requirements_block`). **BUG (both found): `_tool_search_more_evidence` drops `protocol`/`research_frame` → deep-think search runs unscoped. Thread scope through `OutlineWorkspace`.** (accepts `**_ignored` today — clean seam) |
80	| **Compose** | tone/audience/pov into the section advisory-prose slot (prose only); document_type selects skeleton | `multi_section_generator.py:generate_multi_section_report` (:~3010 seams), `_call_section`, `_select_section_system_prompt`. Length = planning context, never truncation. |
81	| **Render** | required sections/order, tables from VERIFIED fields only, references dedup by work, length reported | `compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:main` assembly — thread specs; keep `_audit_citations`. Mark `retrieval_scope_status` when run on a prebuilt corpus. |
82	| **Compliance audit** | term-level satisfied/failed/unsatisfiable/unknown + owning stage; deterministic where possible, one cheap judge for semantic coverage | new `planning/contract_compliance.py:audit_contract`, run AFTER assembly ALONGSIDE (never touching) the frozen faithfulness tripwire. Disclosure-only. |
83	
84	---
85	
86	## 6. Gate ↔ outline-agent seam: **FEED** (both agree)
87	Gate replaces the *pre-retrieval* planning call; the outline agent stays the *post-retrieval evidence-aware refiner*. Refactor `run_outline_agent_or_legacy` into `refine_outline_from_seed(seed_outline, contract, retrieval_projection, coverage_matrix, …)` with the legacy path as `seed = supplied_seed or legacy_call_outline(...)`. Seed behavior depends on provenance: explicit/user headings = immutable title+order; gate-proposed headings = stable `section_id` + revisable title, splittable/mergeable only if every binding term stays mapped. **Required topics are coverage obligations, NOT automatically headings** (the round1 mistake). Extend `OutlineWorkspace` with the contract hash + term ledger; validate every `update_outline` against it (reject dropping an explicit lock or the last owner of a binding term). Degrades to exact champion behavior when the contract names no structure.
88	
89	---
90	
91	## 7. Ask-vs-assume (merged policy)
92	Ask **iff uncertain AND consequential**, ≤3, interactive only. Eligible only if: not already answered, ≥2 plausible interpretations remain, the choice materially changes discovery/structure/decision, and open-leaving wastes real research. Autonomous = **zero asks**, least-restrictive open defaults, every assumption disclosed. Locked defaults: no hard date cutoff ever (prefer-fresher soft, per operator); geography/jurisdiction stay open/comparative; source-type open+prefer-authoritative unless explicit; source-language ≠ prompt language; tone/heading never trigger a question. Explicit "only/must/exclude/do not use" + supplied ranges/counts/headings = hard; "prefer/focus/especially" = soft. When a soft preference starves a mandatory thread → relax + disclose; when two explicit terms conflict → interactive asks, autonomous executes the feasible intersection and labels the rest `UNSATISFIABLE`.
93	
94	---
95	
96	## 8. Build sequence (spikes first; each gated; faithfulness frozen throughout)
97	Work on a fresh branch off `bot/outline-agent-box`. Every step additive + flag-gated (OFF = byte-identical) + measured.
98	
99	- **S0 — Port + reconcile (no behavior change).** Port `round1` rule-reader + tests into champion; put it and the intake extractors behind ONE candidate adapter. Do NOT port the compose-time `filter_eligible` wiring or the coverage-to-heading mapping. *Accept:* candidate reconciliation corpus; OFF path byte-identical.
100	- **S1 — Contract + plan schema + compiler (offline).** Schema, 2 structured calls, deterministic validators. *Accept:* compile all 100 DRB prompts in autonomous mode; human-audit a stratified sample — **no invented hard constraint, no missing explicit clause, an assumption record for every inferred term** (not merely valid JSON).
101	- **S2 — Retrieval projection (the no-starvation proof).** Thread `retrieval_plan`/`research_frame` into FS-Researcher + `run_live_retrieval`; scope into query text + backend routing. *Accept:* on a FRESH task-72 run, telemetry proves journal/English + every mandatory topic reach `_plan_expert_facet_queries` BEFORE results exist; the gate never reduces evidence count vs no-gate (the mechanized 997-row guard).
102	- **S3 — Outline FEED + gap-scope fix.** `refine_outline_from_seed`; seed gap ledger; fix `_tool_search_more_evidence` scope drop. *Accept:* replay banked corpora (tasks 30/61/76/90) — real refinement, locks + term-mappings preserved, gap queries stay in-scope, no second fresh planner.
103	- **S4 — Compose/render projections + compliance audit.** Thread specs; deterministic renderer; `audit_contract`. *Accept:* exact section order / comparison table / length range all trace to verified claims; term-level audit produced.
104	- **S5 — End-to-end fresh autonomous run** on tasks {4,30,61,72,76,90}: retrieval→outline→compose→render, RACE + FACT, **3× per the measurement protocol**. *Accept:* RACE (esp. Instruction-Following) up vs champion, **FACT ≥ 90.3% held**, `provenance_generator.py` clean diff.
105	
106	**Release gate is behavioral** (both insist): the contract must visibly alter FS discovery, survive outline refinement, constrain compose/render, and yield term-level audit results on REAL output. Green unit tests / a filtered fixed corpus / a source-count bump do NOT prove the gate fired.
107	
108	---
109	
110	## 9. Top risks (both designers) + mitigation
111	- **Hallucinated hard constraint** → the `origin==explicit`-for-hard type rule + clause coverage + assumption list.
112	- **Re-introducing the filter trap** → design has NO post-fetch scope filter path; mechanized "gate never lowers evidence count" test.
113	- **Scope loss in outline gap search** → the `_tool_search_more_evidence` fix (both found this bug).
114	- **Two-planner fight** → FEED + required-title subordination already in `apply_revision_ops`.
115	- **Budget truncating a mandatory lane** → compute `mandatory_lane_count` first; expand or fail loudly.
116	- **Compliance-judge circularity** → deterministic checks for counts/headings/length/tables/citations; a separately-configured judge only for semantic coverage; it can NEVER alter faithfulness verdicts.
117	- **Autonomous blocking** → STAGE-2 autonomous is pure `contract→contract`; test asserts zero `needs_input` with no input channel.
118	
119	---
120	
121	*Full independent designs preserved at:* `scratchpad/reviews/sol_gate_design.md` (Sol) and the Fable agent transcript. *Best-practices digest:* `scratchpad/reviews/gate_bestpractices_digest.md`.
122

39:      │    threads, query intents, outline seed,          │
45:      │ retrieval | outline | compose | render | audit    │
50:       (scope first)  outliner    writing    from verified IR
74:**Plan compilation and pinning.** A second structured call decomposes the validated contract into research threads, query intents, and an outline seed. A deterministic compiler creates a coverage matrix from every binding contract term to at least one owning stage and, where evidence is required, at least one retrieval lane. The plan scales to the request: four to six directions are a useful common shape, not a fixed quota. Compound prompts may need more; narrow prompts may need fewer. Query caps may not silently erase mandatory threads. The canonical JSON and SHA-256 approach already present in `src/polaris_graph/planning/research_planner.py:serialize_plan_canonical` and `plan_sha256` should be retained for the richer artifact.
76:**Execution and revision.** The pinned artifact is immutable. Evidence-aware outline refinement may update an execution plan, but it cannot mutate the contract. A legitimate later user change creates a new artifact revision, records a JSON Patch and reason, supersedes the old hash, and invalidates affected downstream checkpoints. Autonomous gap discovery can revise inferred query tactics without changing explicit terms; those tactical revisions are also logged.
78:**Compliance.** Checkpoints audit the contract after retrieval, outline, composition, and render. A final audit reports each term as `SATISFIED`, `FAILED`, `UNSATISFIABLE`, `UNKNOWN`, or `NOT_APPLICABLE`, with output/evidence spans and the responsible stage. Compliance is distinct from factual faithfulness. `src/polaris_graph/generator/provenance_generator.py:strict_verify` and the surrounding provenance engine are frozen: no gate change, remediation rule, or format transformation may weaken, bypass, or edit them.
82:The primary paid-run integration point is `scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py:run_one_query`, before either standard or FS retrieval. `scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:main` starts from a frozen corpus; it can load or autonomously compile a gate artifact to exercise outline/compose/render behavior, but it must label retrieval-scope enforcement as unavailable. Wiring only that script would repeat the known starvation error because no gate scope could influence discovery.
106:Stage = Literal["retrieval", "outline", "compose", "render", "audit"]
324:class OutlineSeed:

1	"""Research Planning Gate feature flags (default-OFF).
2	
3	One tiny module so every stage reads the gate's activation the SAME way and the
4	OFF path is unambiguously byte-identical. Mirrors the champion flag idiom
5	(``fs_researcher_enabled`` etc.): a single ``os.getenv`` read, truthy on
6	``{"1","true","yes","on"}``, default ``"0"`` = OFF.
7	
8	``PG_GATE`` is the S2 master switch: when unset/0, the retrieval-projection
9	threading in ``fs_researcher_query_gen`` / ``live_retriever`` / ``run_one_query``
10	is inert — every ``retrieval_plan`` argument stays ``None`` and the champion path
11	runs unchanged. It gates ONLY whether the gate's projection is CONSULTED; it
12	never itself drops a source or alters faithfulness.
13	"""
14	
15	from __future__ import annotations
16	
17	import os
18	
19	_TRUTHY = ("1", "true", "yes", "on")
20	
21	
22	def gate_enabled() -> bool:
23	    """True iff the planning-gate retrieval projection is wired ON (default OFF)."""
24	    return os.getenv("PG_GATE", "0").strip().lower() in _TRUTHY
25

1	"""Retrieval projection (S2) — the no-starvation core, compile-only + offline.
2	
3	This is the typed *retrieval view* of a pinned :class:`PlanningGateArtifact`
4	(contract + plan). It is the S2 lever the consolidated design (§5, §4 retrieval
5	row) calls out: the gate's scope must reach FS-Researcher **query generation**
6	and backend **routing** BEFORE the first fetch — never as a post-fetch filter of
7	a frozen corpus (the banned 997->131 anti-pattern).
8	
9	A :class:`RetrievalProjection` COMPILES the contract+plan into exactly the three
10	levers ``live_retriever.run_live_retrieval`` already exposes but the driver never
11	populates from the gate:
12	
13	  * ``research_frame``  — a :class:`research_planner.ResearchFrame` whose
14	    ``evidence_needs`` are ROUTED from the contract's ``source_types`` (journal /
15	    academic / scholarly -> ``primary_literature`` = *GO FIND journals*, routing
16	    the S2 / OpenAlex scholarly adapters), plus entities / comparators /
17	    jurisdictions for the field-agnostic need-type registry;
18	  * ``amplified_queries`` — query TEXT built from the plan's MANDATORY query
19	    intents: concepts + required-terms, plus native-language sub-queries for a
20	    hard ``source_languages`` term, plus per-entity sub-queries, plus the hard
21	    scope terms folded into the query text so a "journal-only" rule shapes
22	    discovery from the first query;
23	  * ``protocol`` — the frame's anchor protocol (``to_anchor_protocol``), so the
24	    amplified sub-queries validate against the frame's tokens.
25	
26	Guardrail posture
27	-----------------
28	* **Compile-only. No network, no LLM, no I/O.** This module builds plain data
29	  (lists of strings, a dataclass) from an already-pinned artifact. It NEVER
30	  fetches. The only cross-module import is the champion ``ResearchFrame`` (a pure
31	  dataclass) which it constructs; that construction runs the frame's own
32	  ``validate_evidence_needs`` so a routed need is always a valid enum member.
33	* **ADD / ROUTE, never filter.** Every method returns queries or routing hints
34	  that ENLARGE or STEER discovery. There is no drop path here. The mechanized
35	  997-guard (:func:`candidate_query_count`) counts the candidate query/evidence
36	  lanes so a caller can assert the gate path never REDUCES the lane count vs the
37	  no-gate path — at planning level, before any fetch.
38	* **Hard vs soft.** A HARD scope term (``force==hard``, which the schema
39	  guarantees is explicit/user-backed) is folded into query TEXT and routed to a
40	  backend need. A SOFT term (preference/open) contributes ranking/entity breadth
41	  but is NEVER allowed to gate. This module cannot introduce a hard filter — it
42	  only emits query strings + need tokens.
43	* **Fail-open.** Every builder tolerates a degraded/empty contract: an empty
44	  contract yields an empty projection (no needs, no amplified queries), so the
45	  caller reads the champion default (``research_frame=None`` path) and the run is
46	  byte-identical. Constructing the projection NEVER raises for a shape problem.
47	
48	Nothing here is wired ON by default. ``fs_researcher_query_gen`` /
49	``live_retriever`` / ``run_one_query`` accept a ``retrieval_plan=None`` kwarg
50	(default None => champion behaviour); the projection is threaded only when the
51	``PG_GATE`` flag is set and an artifact exists. See :mod:`gate_flags`.
52	"""
53	
54	from __future__ import annotations
55	
56	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
57	from typing import Any, Optional
58	
59	from src.polaris_graph.planning.planning_gate_schema import (
60	    FORCE_HARD,
61	    PlanningGateArtifact,
62	    QueryIntent,
63	    ResearchContract,
64	    ResearchExecutionPlan,
65	)
66	
67	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
68	# source_type -> evidence_need routing (the "GO FIND journals" map)
69	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
70	# Maps a contract ``scope.source_types`` value (free-string, model-emitted) onto
71	# the field-agnostic ``EVIDENCE_NEEDS`` enum the need-type router routes adapters
72	# off. This is the load-bearing S2 routing: a "journal articles only" contract
73	# term (task-72) routes ``primary_literature`` = the S2 + OpenAlex + scholarly-
74	# graph adapters, so FS goes and FINDS journals from the first query instead of
75	# filtering a general corpus afterwards.
76	#
77	# Each key is matched as a case-insensitive SUBSTRING of the normalized source
78	# type, so "peer-reviewed journal article", "academic paper", "scholarly
79	# article" all route primary_literature. Unmatched source types contribute no
80	# need (the router's safe generic fallback applies), never an error.
81	_SOURCE_TYPE_NEED_MAP: tuple[tuple[str, str], ...] = (
82	    ("journal", "primary_literature"),
83	    ("peer-review", "primary_literature"),
84	    ("peer review", "primary_literature"),
85	    ("academic", "primary_literature"),
86	    ("scholarly", "primary_literature"),
87	    ("scientific", "primary_literature"),
88	    ("paper", "primary_literature"),
89	    ("study", "primary_literature"),
90	    ("preprint", "primary_literature"),
91	    ("regulation", "regulatory"),
92	    ("regulatory", "regulatory"),
93	    ("guideline", "regulatory"),
94	    ("law", "legal"),
95	    ("legal", "legal"),
96	    ("case law", "legal"),
97	    ("statute", "legal"),
98	    ("court", "legal"),
99	    ("statistic", "statistical"),
100	    ("census", "statistical"),
101	    ("survey data", "statistical"),
102	    ("dataset", "datasets"),
103	    ("standard", "standards"),
104	    ("news", "news_press"),
105	    ("press", "news_press"),
106	    ("media", "news_press"),
107	    ("filing", "company_filings"),
108	    ("sec ", "company_filings"),
109	    ("10-k", "company_filings"),
110	    ("annual report", "company_filings"),
111	)
112	
113	
114	def _norm(s: Any) -> str:
115	    return str(s or "").strip().lower()
116	
117	
118	def source_type_to_need(source_type: Any) -> Optional[str]:
119	    """Route a single contract ``source_type`` value onto an ``EVIDENCE_NEEDS``
120	    token, or ``None`` if it matches no known family (the router falls back to
121	    the safe generic set — never an error)."""
122	    st = _norm(source_type)
123	    if not st:
124	        return None
125	    for needle, need in _SOURCE_TYPE_NEED_MAP:
126	        if needle in st:
127	            return need
128	    return None
129

131	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
132	# The projection
133	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
134	
135	
136	@dataclass
137	class RetrievalProjection:
138	    """Compile-time retrieval view of a pinned contract + plan.
139	
140	    Built by :func:`from_artifact` (or :meth:`ResearchContract.to_research_frame`
141	    via the thin methods added to the schema). Holds only plain data; the
142	    ``research_frame`` is materialized lazily by :meth:`to_research_frame` so this
143	    dataclass has no heavy import at construction.
144	    """
145	
146	    contract: ResearchContract
147	    plan: ResearchExecutionPlan
148	    original_prompt: str = ""
149	
150	    # Precomputed routing tokens (validated against the frame enum lazily).
151	    evidence_needs: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
152	    jurisdictions: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
153	    entities: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
154	    comparators: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
155	    # Hard scope-term VALUES (verbatim), folded into query text + surfaced for a
156	    # backend-native filter by a caller that supports one. NEVER a drop here.
157	    hard_scope_terms: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
158	    # Soft/preference scope-term values — ranking hints only, never a gate.
159	    soft_scope_terms: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
160	    # Hard source-language codes/names (e.g. "en"); drive native-language lanes.
161	    hard_languages: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
162	
163	    # -- amplified query text (the FS frontier seed) --------------------------
164	
165	    def to_amplified_queries(self, *, base_question: str = "") -> list[str]:
166	        """The gate's query TEXT for ``run_live_retrieval(amplified_queries=...)``.
167	
168	        Built from the plan's MANDATORY query intents (concepts + required
169	        terms), each scope-anchored by folding the hard scope terms into the
170	        query string so the FIRST query already carries "journal" / "English"
171	        etc. Then per-entity sub-queries and native-language lanes are ADDED.
172	        Order-stable, de-duplicated (case-insensitively), never truncated here.
173	
174	        This is strictly ADDITIVE: it returns candidate query strings; the caller
175	        (FS-Researcher / live_retriever) runs each through the UNCHANGED
176	        per-query retrieval + frozen faithfulness engine. Nothing is dropped.
177	        """
178	        out: list[str] = []
179	        seen: set[str] = set()
180	
181	        def _add(q: str) -> None:
182	            qs = (q or "").strip()
183	            if not qs:
184	                return
185	            key = qs.lower()
186	            if key in seen:
187	                return
188	            seen.add(key)
189	            out.append(qs)
190	
191	        scope_suffix = " ".join(self.hard_scope_terms).strip()
192	
193	        for qi in self.plan.mandatory_query_intents():
194	            for text in _intent_query_texts(qi):
195	                if scope_suffix and scope_suffix.lower() not in text.lower():
196	                    _add(f"{text} {scope_suffix}")
197	                else:
198	                    _add(text)
199	
200	        # per-entity sub-queries (sub-entity discovery), anchored on the base
201	        # question so a bare entity does not drift into its broad field.
202	        anchor = (base_question or self.original_prompt or "").strip()
203	        for ent in self.entities:
204	            ent = (ent or "").strip()
205	            if not ent:
206	                continue
207	            if anchor:
208	                _add(f"{ent} {anchor}")
209	            else:
210	                _add(ent)
211	
212	        # native-language lanes: for a HARD source-language, ADD the scope-anchored
213	        # query in that language slot (the routing hint; the multilingual lane in
214	        # FS translates). We surface a language-tagged variant of the first few
215	        # mandatory concepts so a "source: English-language" rule reaches the
216	        # multilingual reserve as an explicit lane rather than being inferred.
217	        # (English is the pipeline default; a non-English hard language adds a
218	        # native tag so the reserve fires.)
219	        for lang in self.hard_languages:
220	            lang = (lang or "").strip()
221	            if not lang or lang.lower() in ("en", "english"):
222	                continue
223	            for qi in self.plan.mandatory_query_intents()[:2]:
224	                for text in _intent_query_texts(qi):
225	                    _add(f"{text} ({lang})")
226	
227	        return out
228	
229	    # -- scope terms ----------------------------------------------------------
230	
231	    def to_scope_terms(self) -> dict[str, list[str]]:
232	        """The hard/soft scope split + language lanes, as plain lists.
233	
234	        A caller that supports a backend-native filter reads ``hard`` for the
235	        explicit prohibitions/requirements (journal-only, English-only) and
236	        ``soft`` for ranking preferences. This is a compile view — it never
237	        applies a filter itself. The 997-guard reads the union count.
238	        """
239	        return {
240	            "hard": list(self.hard_scope_terms),
241	            "soft": list(self.soft_scope_terms),
242	            "languages": list(self.hard_languages),
243	            "evidence_needs": list(self.evidence_needs),
244	        }
245	
246	    # -- research frame (backend routing) -------------------------------------
247	
248	    def to_research_frame(self) -> Any:
249	        """Materialize a champion :class:`ResearchFrame` for backend routing.
250	
251	        ``evidence_needs`` route the need-type registry (primary_literature ->
252	        S2/OpenAlex scholarly adapters = GO FIND journals). Entities/comparators
253	        seed the anchor tokens. Jurisdictions route jurisdiction scopes. Runs the
254	        frame's own ``validate_evidence_needs`` so a routed need is always a valid
255	        enum member; an unroutable source type simply contributes no need (safe
256	        generic fallback), never an error.
257	
258	        Returns ``None`` when the projection carries NOTHING to route (empty
259	        needs AND no entities/jurisdictions) so the caller keeps the champion
260	        ``research_frame=None`` (byte-identical) path.
261	        """
262	        if not (self.evidence_needs or self.entities or self.jurisdictions
263	                or self.comparators):
264	            return None
265	        from src.polaris_graph.planning.research_planner import (  # noqa: PLC0415
266	            ResearchFrame,
267	            validate_evidence_needs,
268	            validate_jurisdiction_shapes,
269	        )
270	        # validate/normalize; drop any that fail SHAPE rather than raise (compile
271	        # view is fail-open — a bad token contributes nothing, never aborts).
272	        try:
273	            needs = validate_evidence_needs(self.evidence_needs)
274	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
275	            needs = [n for n in self.evidence_needs
276	                     if _safe_need(n)]
277	        try:
278	            juris = validate_jurisdiction_shapes(self.jurisdictions)
279	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
280	            juris = []
281	        return ResearchFrame(
282	            entities=list(self.entities),
283	            comparators=list(self.comparators),
284	            constraints=list(self.hard_scope_terms),
285	            evidence_needs=needs,
286	            jurisdictions=juris,
287	        )
288	
289	    def to_protocol(self, *, base_question: str = "") -> Optional[dict[str, Any]]:
290	        """The anchor protocol for ``validate_amplified_queries`` (from the frame).
291	
292	        ``None`` when there is no frame to anchor (keeps the champion path)."""
293	        frame = self.to_research_frame()
294	        if frame is None:
295	            return None
296	        anchor = (base_question or self.original_prompt or "").strip()
297	        return frame.to_anchor_protocol(anchor)
298	
299	    # -- the mechanized 997-guard (planning level) ----------------------------
300	
301	    def candidate_query_count(self, *, base_question: str = "") -> int:
302	        """Count the candidate query/evidence lanes this projection ADDS.
303	
304	        The 997-guard: a caller asserts ``gate_count >= no_gate_count`` so the
305	        gate path can NEVER reduce the candidate query/evidence-need count vs the
306	        no-gate path — at PLANNING level, before any fetch. Counts distinct
307	        amplified queries plus routed evidence needs (each a discovery lane)."""
308	        return (
309	            len(self.to_amplified_queries(base_question=base_question))
310	            + len(self.evidence_needs)
311	        )
312	
313	
314	def _safe_need(n: Any) -> bool:
315	    from src.polaris_graph.planning.research_planner import EVIDENCE_NEEDS  # noqa: PLC0415
316	    return _norm(n) in EVIDENCE_NEEDS
317	
318	
319	def _intent_query_texts(qi: QueryIntent) -> list[str]:
320	    """The query string(s) a single :class:`QueryIntent` contributes.
321	
322	    Prefers a concept+required-term join; falls back to alternate phrasings, then
323	    the raw thread question via concepts. Always returns at least one non-empty
324	    string when the intent carries any text."""
325	    parts: list[str] = []
326	    core = " ".join([*qi.concepts, *qi.required_terms]).strip()
327	    if core:
328	        parts.append(core)
329	    for alt in qi.alternate_phrasings:
330	        alt = (alt or "").strip()
331	        if alt:
332	            parts.append(alt)
333	    if not parts:
334	        # nothing structured — fall back to any single concept/phrasing.
335	        for cand in [*qi.concepts, *qi.alternate_phrasings]:
336	            if (cand or "").strip():
337	                parts.append(cand.strip())
338	                break
339	    return parts
340	
341	
342	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
343	# Compilation from a pinned artifact / contract
344	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
345	
346	# Scope dimensions whose VALUES are folded into query text as scope terms.
347	_SCOPE_TEXT_DIMENSIONS: tuple[str, ...] = (
348	    "scope.source_types",
349	    "scope.domains",
350	    "scope.geographies",
351	    "scope.prohibited",  # a prohibition is disclosed as a term the caller may filter
352	)
353	_LANGUAGE_DIMENSIONS: tuple[str, ...] = (
354	    "scope.source_languages",
355	    "scope.languages",
356	)
357	
358	
359	def from_contract_and_plan(
360	    contract: ResearchContract,
361	    plan: ResearchExecutionPlan,
362	    *,
363	    original_prompt: str = "",
364	) -> RetrievalProjection:
365	    """Compile a :class:`RetrievalProjection` from a contract + plan.
366	
367	    Pure + deterministic. Walks the contract's scope + content terms and the
368	    plan's query intents to derive routing tokens. Fail-open: a degraded/empty
369	    contract yields an empty projection (the caller keeps the champion path).
370	    """
371	    evidence_needs: list[str] = []
372	    jurisdictions: list[str] = []
373	    entities: list[str] = []
374	    comparators: list[str] = []
375	    hard_scope_terms: list[str] = []
376	    soft_scope_terms: list[str] = []
377	    hard_languages: list[str] = []
378	
379	    def _dedup_add(lst: list[str], val: Any) -> None:
380	        s = str(val or "").strip()
381	        if not s:
382	            return
383	        if s.lower() not in {x.lower() for x in lst}:
384	            lst.append(s)
385	
386	    for term in contract.scope:
387	        dim = _norm(term.dimension)
388	        val = term.value
389	        if val in (None, "", [], {}):
390	            continue
391	        vals = val if isinstance(val, (list, tuple)) else [val]
392	
393	        if dim in _LANGUAGE_DIMENSIONS:
394	            if term.force == FORCE_HARD:
395	                for v in vals:
396	                    _dedup_add(hard_languages, v)
397	            continue
398	
399	        # source_types route evidence needs (the GO-FIND-journals map).
400	        if dim == "scope.source_types":
401	            for v in vals:
402	                need = source_type_to_need(v)
403	                if need:
404	                    _dedup_add(evidence_needs, need)
405	
406	        if dim in ("scope.jurisdictions", "scope.geographies"):
407	            for v in vals:
408	                _dedup_add(jurisdictions, str(v).strip().upper()[:4])
409	
410	        # fold the value into the hard/soft scope text bucket.
411	        bucket = hard_scope_terms if term.force == FORCE_HARD else soft_scope_terms
412	        if dim in _SCOPE_TEXT_DIMENSIONS or term.force == FORCE_HARD:
413	            for v in vals:
414	                _dedup_add(bucket, v)
415	
416	    # entities + comparators from the content spec (sub-entity discovery breadth).
417	    for ent in contract.content_terms:
418	        if _norm(ent.dimension).startswith("content.entit") and ent.value:
419	            _dedup_add(entities, ent.value)
420	    for cov in contract.coverage:
421	        if cov.kind in ("entity",) and cov.statement.value:
422	            _dedup_add(entities, cov.statement.value)
423	        if cov.kind in ("comparison",):
424	            for c in cov.compare_on:
425	                if c.value:
426	                    _dedup_add(comparators, c.value)
427	
428	    return RetrievalProjection(
429	        contract=contract,
430	        plan=plan,
431	        original_prompt=original_prompt,
432	        evidence_needs=evidence_needs,
433	        jurisdictions=jurisdictions,
434	        entities=entities,
435	        comparators=comparators,
436	        hard_scope_terms=hard_scope_terms,
437	        soft_scope_terms=soft_scope_terms,
438	        hard_languages=hard_languages,
439	    )
440	
441	
442	def from_artifact(artifact: PlanningGateArtifact) -> RetrievalProjection:
443	    """Compile a :class:`RetrievalProjection` from a pinned artifact."""
444	    return from_contract_and_plan(
445	        artifact.contract,
446	        artifact.plan,
447	        original_prompt=artifact.original_prompt,
448	    )

451	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
452	# Transitional adapter: champion ResearchPlan -> projection (the FS seam fix)
453	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
454	
455	
456	class _ChampionPlanProjection(RetrievalProjection):
457	    """A projection whose amplified queries ARE the champion plan's ``sub_queries``.
458	
459	    S2 seam fix (design §5): the FS-Researcher branch in ``run_one_query``
460	    currently passes only ``_clean_question`` and DISCARDS
461	    ``_research_plan.sub_queries``. This adapter wraps a champion
462	    ``research_planner.ResearchPlan`` as a :class:`RetrievalProjection` so the FS
463	    frontier is seeded with those sub-queries (the gate's pre-retrieval lanes)
464	    plus the frame's ``evidence_needs`` routing — BEFORE any fetch. Until the full
465	    gate artifact is wired into ``run_one_query`` (a later step), this carries the
466	    already-computed champion plan into the FS query frontier instead of throwing
467	    it away. It NEVER drops a source; it only ADDS the sub-queries the FS branch
468	    was discarding, and only when ``PG_GATE`` is ON.
469	    """
470	
471	    def __init__(self, research_plan: Any, *, original_prompt: str = "") -> None:
472	        frame = getattr(research_plan, "frame", None)
473	        sub_queries = list(getattr(research_plan, "sub_queries", []) or [])
474	        super().__init__(
475	            contract=ResearchContract(),
476	            plan=ResearchExecutionPlan(),
477	            original_prompt=original_prompt,
478	            evidence_needs=list(getattr(frame, "evidence_needs", []) or []),
479	            jurisdictions=list(getattr(frame, "jurisdictions", []) or []),
480	            entities=list(getattr(frame, "entities", []) or []),
481	            comparators=list(getattr(frame, "comparators", []) or []),
482	        )
483	        self._sub_queries = sub_queries
484	        self._frame = frame
485	
486	    def to_amplified_queries(self, *, base_question: str = "") -> list[str]:
487	        out: list[str] = []
488	        seen: set[str] = set()
489	        for q in self._sub_queries:
490	            qs = (q or "").strip()
491	            if qs and qs.lower() not in seen:
492	                seen.add(qs.lower())
493	                out.append(qs)
494	        return out
495	
496	    def to_research_frame(self) -> Any:
497	        return self._frame
498	
499	    def to_protocol(self, *, base_question: str = "") -> Optional[dict[str, Any]]:
500	        if self._frame is None:
501	            return None
502	        try:
503	            return self._frame.to_anchor_protocol(
504	                (base_question or self.original_prompt or "").strip()
505	            )
506	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
507	            return None
508	
509	
510	def from_champion_plan(
511	    research_plan: Any, *, original_prompt: str = ""
512	) -> Optional[RetrievalProjection]:
513	    """Adapt a champion ``ResearchPlan`` into a projection, or ``None`` if it has

563	class ResearchContract:
564	    """The typed contract. Grouped as objective / scope / content / deliverable.
565	
566	    To keep the schema compact and fail-soft, the grouped specs are held as flat
567	    ``ContractTerm`` lists keyed by ``dimension`` (e.g. ``objective.question``,
568	    ``scope.source_types``, ``scope.date``, ``deliverable.kind``) plus the two
569	    structured collections that need their own shape (``coverage`` and
570	    ``sections``). Every term is uniformly a :class:`ContractTerm`, so the
571	    validator's no-invention sweep is a single pass over :meth:`all_terms`.
572	    """
573	
574	    schema_version: str = SCHEMA_VERSION
575	    objective: list[ContractTerm] = field(default_factory=list)
576	    scope: list[ContractTerm] = field(default_factory=list)
577	    content_terms: list[ContractTerm] = field(default_factory=list)
578	    deliverable: list[ContractTerm] = field(default_factory=list)
579	    coverage: list[CoverageRequirement] = field(default_factory=list)
580	    sections: list[SectionRequirement] = field(default_factory=list)
581	    ambiguities: list[Ambiguity] = field(default_factory=list)
582	    assumptions: list[Assumption] = field(default_factory=list)
583	    conflicts: list[ContractConflict] = field(default_factory=list)
584	    complexity: str = "standard"
585	    compiler_degraded: bool = False
586	
587	    def all_terms(self) -> list[ContractTerm]:
588	        """Every :class:`ContractTerm` in the contract (for the validator sweep)."""
589	        terms: list[ContractTerm] = []
590	        terms.extend(self.objective)
591	        terms.extend(self.scope)
592	        terms.extend(self.content_terms)
593	        terms.extend(self.deliverable)
594	        for cr in self.coverage:
595	            terms.append(cr.statement)
596	            terms.extend(cr.compare_on)
597	        for sec in self.sections:
598	            terms.append(sec.title)
599	        return terms
600	
601	    def hard_terms(self) -> list[ContractTerm]:
602	        return [t for t in self.all_terms() if t.is_hard()]
603	
604	    # -- S2 retrieval-projection compile methods ------------------------------
605	    # Thin wrappers over ``retrieval_projection`` (the no-starvation core). Kept
606	    # on the contract per the S2 task ("contract.to_research_frame() /
607	    # to_scope_terms() compile methods"); they build a projection over THIS
608	    # contract with an empty plan (scope routing needs only the contract) so a
609	    # caller holding just a contract can still route backends. The full
610	    # query-text projection (which needs the plan's mandatory intents) is built
611	    # via ``retrieval_projection.from_artifact`` / ``from_contract_and_plan``.
612	    # Deferred import keeps this module free of the ResearchFrame dependency.
613	
614	    def to_research_frame(self, plan: Any = None) -> Any:
615	        """Route this contract's scope onto a champion ``ResearchFrame`` (or
616	        ``None`` when nothing is routable — the byte-identical champion path)."""
617	        from src.polaris_graph.planning.retrieval_projection import (  # noqa: PLC0415
618	            from_contract_and_plan,
619	        )
620	        proj = from_contract_and_plan(self, plan or ResearchExecutionPlan())
621	        return proj.to_research_frame()
622	
623	    def to_scope_terms(self, plan: Any = None) -> dict[str, list[str]]:
624	        """The hard/soft scope split + language lanes + routed evidence needs."""
625	        from src.polaris_graph.planning.retrieval_projection import (  # noqa: PLC0415
626	            from_contract_and_plan,
627	        )
628	        proj = from_contract_and_plan(self, plan or ResearchExecutionPlan())
629	        return proj.to_scope_terms()
630	
631	    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
632	        return {
633	            "schema_version": self.schema_version,
634	            "objective": [t.to_dict() for t in self.objective],
635	            "scope": [t.to_dict() for t in self.scope],
636	            "content_terms": [t.to_dict() for t in self.content_terms],
637	            "deliverable": [t.to_dict() for t in self.deliverable],
638	            "coverage": [c.to_dict() for c in self.coverage],
639	            "sections": [s.to_dict() for s in self.sections],
640	            "ambiguities": [a.to_dict() for a in self.ambiguities],
641	            "assumptions": [a.to_dict() for a in self.assumptions],
642	            "conflicts": [c.to_dict() for c in self.conflicts],
643	            "complexity": self.complexity,
644	            "compiler_degraded": self.compiler_degraded,
645	        }
646	
647	
648	def contract_from_dict(d: Any) -> ResearchContract:
649	    """Parse a compiler JSON object (fail-soft) into a :class:`ResearchContract`.
650	
651	    Accepts either the flat shape this module emits, or the grouped shape a
652	    compiler might emit (``objective``/``scope``/``content``/``deliverable``
653	    objects whose leaves are terms). Missing groups → empty (open) contract.
654	    """
655	    if not isinstance(d, dict):
656	        raise SchemaError(f"contract must be an object, got {type(d).__name__}")
657	
658	    def _terms(key: str, *, group_prefix: str) -> list[ContractTerm]:
659	        raw = d.get(key)
660	        out: list[ContractTerm] = []
661	        if isinstance(raw, list):
662	            for item in raw:
663	                if isinstance(item, dict):
664	                    out.append(ContractTerm.from_dict(item))
665	        elif isinstance(raw, dict):
666	            # grouped object: {dimension_suffix: term-or-value}
667	            for sub_key, sub_val in raw.items():
668	                if isinstance(sub_val, dict) and "origin" in sub_val:
669	                    t = ContractTerm.from_dict(sub_val)
670	                    if not t.dimension:
671	                        t.dimension = f"{group_prefix}.{sub_key}"
672	                    if not t.term_id:
673	                        t.term_id = f"{group_prefix}.{sub_key}"
674	                    out.append(t)
675	                elif sub_val is not None:
676	                    out.append(ContractTerm(
677	                        term_id=f"{group_prefix}.{sub_key}",
678	                        dimension=f"{group_prefix}.{sub_key}",
679	                        value=sub_val,

318	    status: str = "PENDING"          # PENDING | IN_PROGRESS | COMPLETE | UNFILLED

327	class GapLedger:
328	    """Dedup-by-(section,aspect) EXACT match, falling back to same-section token-overlap
329	    (Jaccard >= threshold) paraphrase collapse; per-aspect retry cap; UNFILLED + disclosed
330	    on exhaustion.
331	
332	    §-1.3.1 / §-1.1: this ledger is a ROUTING signal (what to search next), never a quality
333	    verdict about the corpus — it names todos, it does not score the pipeline's own output.
334	    """
335	
336	    def __init__(self, max_retries_per_aspect: Optional[int] = None):
337	        self._todos: dict[tuple[str, str], GapTodo] = {}
338	        self._max_retries = (
339	            max_retries_per_aspect
340	            if max_retries_per_aspect is not None
341	            else _gap_retries_per_aspect()
342	        )
343	
344	    def _find_paraphrase(self, section: str, aspect: str) -> Optional[GapTodo]:
345	        """Same-section, token-overlap match against an EXISTING todo (any status). Iter-2
346	        P1-1: collapses re-worded checklist gaps onto the one todo already tracking that
347	        aspect so the per-aspect retry cap actually bites."""
348	        canon_new = _canon_tokens(aspect)
349	        if not canon_new:
350	            return None
351	        threshold = _aspect_dedup_threshold()
352	        best: Optional[GapTodo] = None
353	        best_score = 0.0
354	        for (sec2, asp2), todo in self._todos.items():
355	            if sec2 != section:
356	                continue
357	            score = _jaccard(canon_new, _canon_tokens(asp2))
358	            if score >= threshold and score > best_score:
359	                best, best_score = todo, score
360	        return best
361	
362	    def add(
363	        self, section: str, aspect: str,
364	        needed_kind: str = "coverage", source: str = "checklist",
365	    ) -> GapTodo:
366	        section = str(section or "").strip()
367	        aspect = str(aspect or "").strip()
368	        key = (section, aspect)
369	        existing = self._todos.get(key)
370	        if existing is not None:
371	            return existing
372	        paraphrase = self._find_paraphrase(section, aspect)
373	        if paraphrase is not None:
374	            return paraphrase
375	        todo = GapTodo(
376	            section=section, aspect=aspect, needed_kind=needed_kind, source=source,
377	        )
378	        self._todos[key] = todo
379	        return todo
380	
381	    def add_unfillable(
382	        self, section: str, aspect: str, reason: str,
383	        needed_kind: str = "coverage", source: str = "checklist",
384	    ) -> GapTodo:
385	        """Iter-3 P0 fix (real-corpus degeneration — 'Summary Table' gap re-fired ~10x over
386	        945s while every fetched row stayed orphaned in the pool): a gap whose named section
387	        can NEVER be routed to retrieval — it does not match any current outline title, and
388	        under a locked required-structure deliverable there is no way to add a new section for
389	        it — is a STRUCTURAL limitation, not a content gap. Recording it UNFILLED immediately
390	        (never PENDING) means ``next_pending()`` never selects it and the decide-loop never
391	        fires ``search_more_evidence`` for it, so the loop cannot burn its turn/wall budget
392	        re-fetching the same unhomeable aspect under a slightly different checklist wording
393	        each round. Still disclosed (§-1.3 — no silent drop), just never retried."""
394	        todo = self.add(section=section, aspect=aspect, needed_kind=needed_kind, source=source)
395	        if todo.status not in ("UNFILLED", "COMPLETE"):
396	            todo.status = "UNFILLED"
397	            todo.disclosure = reason
398	        return todo
399	
400	    def get(self, section: str, aspect: str) -> Optional[GapTodo]:
401	        return self._todos.get((str(section or "").strip(), str(aspect or "").strip()))
402	
403	    def next_pending(self) -> Optional[GapTodo]:
404	        for todo in self._todos.values():
405	            if todo.status == "PENDING":
406	                return todo
407	        return None
408	
409	    def mark_in_progress(self, todo: GapTodo) -> None:
410	        todo.status = "IN_PROGRESS"
411	        todo.attempts += 1
412	
413	    def mark_complete(self, todo: GapTodo) -> None:
414	        todo.status = "COMPLETE"

416	    def mark_retry_or_unfilled(self, todo: GapTodo, reason: str) -> None:
417	        """Per-aspect retry: PENDING again while attempts < cap; UNFILLED + disclosed at cap."""
418	        if todo.attempts >= self._max_retries:
419	            todo.status = "UNFILLED"
420	            todo.disclosure = reason
421	        else:
422	            todo.status = "PENDING"
423

427

446	                f"kind={t.needed_kind} attempts={t.attempts}/{self._max_retries} source={t.source}"
447	            )

487	    # registry verifies its token (verify_modeled_atom), so emission stays fail-closed and a

I now have a full understanding. Let me design the S3 implementation.

## Design summary

The refactor is entirely additive and flag-gated. Key elements:

1. **`refine_outline_from_seed(...)`** — a new keyword-only entry point that accepts `seed_outline` (pre-built SectionPlan list), `contract`, `retrieval_projection`, `coverage_matrix`. `run_outline_agent_or_legacy` becomes a thin adapter: builds the seed via `_call_outline` (or uses supplied seed) then delegates. OFF path unchanged.

2. **FEED**: pre-load `required_coverage`/`must_address` (coverage obligations from the contract) as PENDING gaps — NOT headings. Explicit headings → title+order lock via existing `required_titles` path.

3. **Bug fix**: thread scope projection (research_frame + protocol) into `_tool_search_more_evidence`'s `run_live_retrieval` call, via new OutlineWorkspace fields.

4. **OutlineWorkspace** gains: `contract_hash`, `term_ledger` (binding term → owning sections), `gate_research_frame`, `gate_protocol`. `_tool_update_outline` validates against the ledger (reject dropping an explicit lock / last owner of a binding term).

All new behavior behind a `None`-default kwarg (`gate_projection=None`), so OFF path is byte-identical.

"""Outline FEED — the gate ↔ outline-agent seam (S3), additive + default-OFF.

The Research Planning Gate produces ONE pinned :class:`PlanningGateArtifact`
(contract + plan). At the *post-retrieval* outline stage the gate does not
replace the evidence-aware outline agent; it **FEEDS** it (consolidated design
§6, Sol §5): it pre-loads the contract's coverage obligations as PENDING gaps so
the deep-think loop CLOSES contract gaps, and it threads the retrieval *scope*
(the same ``research_frame`` / ``protocol`` used by initial retrieval) into the
outline-stage gap search so a correctly scoped first pass is not diluted by an
unconstrained follow-up query.

This module is PURE + deterministic (no network, no LLM, no I/O). It only reads
an already-pinned contract/projection and returns plain data:

  * :func:`coverage_gap_seeds` — the contract's REQUIRED coverage obligations
    (``required_coverage`` / ``must_address``) as ``(section, aspect)`` gap
    seeds. **Required topics are coverage OBLIGATIONS, not automatically
    headings** — they are pre-loaded PENDING in the gap ledger, never mapped 1:1
    onto section titles (the round-1 coverage-to-heading bug the design forbids).
  * :func:`build_term_ledger` — the binding-term → owning-section map + the set
    of exact-title / order locks, so ``update_outline`` can reject a revision
    that drops an explicit lock or the last owner of a binding term.
  * :func:`gate_scope_for_gap_search` — the ``(research_frame, protocol)`` pair a
    :class:`RetrievalProjection` compiles, threaded into the outline-stage gap
    search (:func:`outline_agent._tool_search_more_evidence`).

Guardrail posture
-----------------
* **ADD / ROUTE, never filter.** The FEED only ADDS PENDING gap todos (search
  MORE) and ROUTES the gap search to the gate's scope. It never drops a source,
  never filters the banked corpus, never removes a section that owns a binding
  term. There is no drop path here.
* **Coverage != heading.** Required coverage becomes a gap OBLIGATION with an
  ``(section="", aspect=<topic>)`` unassigned home, so the loop searches for it
  and the outliner homes it under whatever section fits — the topic is never
  forced to become its own heading.
* **Fail-open.** Every helper tolerates a degraded/empty/``None`` contract or
  projection: it returns empty data, so the caller reads the champion default
  (no seeded gaps, no ledger locks, no scoped gap search) and the run is
  byte-identical to today's champion.
* **Never invent a constraint.** Only ``force == hard`` terms (which the schema
  guarantees are ``origin == explicit``/user-backed) become binding-term ledger
  owners or locks. Inferred / preference terms contribute optional gap seeds
  only — never a lock, never a hard veto on a revision.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Any, Optional

# Keep this module import-light: the schema types are only used for isinstance /
# attribute reads, all guarded, so a caller may pass duck-typed objects too.

# Coverage-requirement dimensions/keys that name a REQUIRED obligation. A gate
# contract carries these as ``CoverageRequirement`` objects; a looser upstream
# may hand a plain dict with ``required_coverage`` / ``must_address`` lists.
_REQUIRED_COVERAGE_KEYS: tuple[str, ...] = ("required_coverage", "must_address")


def _norm(s: Any) -> str:
    return str(s or "").strip()


def _term_value_text(term: Any) -> str:
    """The human-readable text of a ContractTerm-ish object (or plain value)."""
    if term is None:
        return ""
    val = getattr(term, "value", term)
    if isinstance(val, (list, tuple)):
        return " ".join(_norm(v) for v in val if _norm(v)).strip()
    return _norm(val)


def _is_hard(term: Any) -> bool:
    """True iff the term is a HARD force term (schema guarantees explicit/user)."""
    force = getattr(term, "force", None)
    if force is not None:
        return str(force).strip().lower() == "hard"
    # duck-typed dict shape
    if isinstance(term, dict):
        return str(term.get("force", "")).strip().lower() == "hard"
    return False


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# FEED 1 — coverage obligations -> PENDING gap seeds (NOT headings)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def coverage_gap_seeds(contract: Any) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
    """The contract's REQUIRED coverage obligations as gap seeds.

    Returns a list of ``{"section": ..., "aspect": ..., "term_id": ...}``. Each
    is a coverage OBLIGATION the deep-think loop should CLOSE — pre-loaded PENDING
    in the gap ledger by the caller, so ``next_pending()`` routes a scoped gap
    search for it. **The ``section`` is deliberately left EMPTY ("(unassigned)")**
    unless the coverage requirement is bound to an EXPLICIT section: a required
    topic is a coverage obligation, not automatically a heading (the round-1 bug).

    Fail-open: a ``None`` / empty / shapeless contract yields ``[]`` (no seeds),
    so the caller keeps the champion behavior (no pre-loaded gaps).
    """
    if contract is None:
        return []
    out: list[dict[str, str]] = []
    seen: set[str] = set()

    def _add(aspect: str, term_id: str = "", section: str = "") -> None:
        aspect = _norm(aspect)
        if not aspect:
            return
        key = f"{_norm(section).lower()}||{aspect.lower()}"
        if key in seen:
            return
        seen.add(key)
        out.append({
            "section": _norm(section),
            "aspect": aspect,
            "term_id": _norm(term_id),
        })

    # 1) Structured contract: CoverageRequirement objects marked required.
    coverage = getattr(contract, "coverage", None)
    if isinstance(coverage, (list, tuple)):
        # map requirement_id -> the explicit section that owns it (if any), so a
        # coverage req the deliverable EXPLICITLY bound to a section keeps that
        # home; every other required topic stays unassigned (never a new heading).
        req_to_section = _explicit_req_sections(contract)
        for cr in coverage:
            required = getattr(cr, "required", True)
            if not required:
                continue
            aspect = _term_value_text(getattr(cr, "statement", None))
            if not aspect:
                # fall back to the requirement's own kind/id so it is still tracked
                aspect = _norm(getattr(cr, "requirement_id", ""))
            req_id = _norm(getattr(cr, "requirement_id", ""))
            term_id = _norm(getattr(getattr(cr, "statement", None), "term_id", "")) or req_id
            _add(aspect, term_id=term_id, section=req_to_section.get(req_id, ""))

    # 2) Duck-typed dict shape (a looser upstream): required_coverage/must_address.
    if isinstance(contract, dict):
        for key in _REQUIRED_COVERAGE_KEYS:
            vals = contract.get(key)
            if isinstance(vals, (list, tuple)):
                for v in vals:
                    _add(_term_value_text(v) or _norm(v))

    return out


def _explicit_req_sections(contract: Any) -> dict[str, str]:
    """Map ``requirement_id -> explicit section title`` for coverage reqs the
    deliverable EXPLICITLY placed under an exact-title-locked section. Only an
    explicit / user section counts (never an inferred/gate-proposed heading), so
    a required topic never silently becomes a heading."""
    out: dict[str, str] = {}
    sections = getattr(contract, "sections", None)
    if not isinstance(sections, (list, tuple)):
        return out
    for sec in sections:
        if not getattr(sec, "exact_title_lock", False):
            continue
        title = _term_value_text(getattr(sec, "title", None))
        if not title:
            continue
        for rid in (getattr(sec, "coverage_requirement_ids", None) or []):
            rid = _norm(rid)
            if rid:
                out[rid] = title
    return out


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# FEED 2 — the term ledger (binding term -> owning sections + locks)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def build_term_ledger(
    contract: Any, coverage_matrix: Any = None,
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Build the immutable term ledger the outline workspace validates against.

    Returns a plain dict::

        {
          "binding_term_owners": {term_id: [section_title, ...]},  # last-owner guard
          "locked_titles": [title, ...],       # exact-title locks (explicit/user)
          "ordered_titles": [title, ...],      # order-locked titles (in order)
          "binding_term_ids": [term_id, ...],  # every hard/binding term id
        }

    Only HARD (``force == hard``, schema-guaranteed explicit/user) terms are
    binding. Section ownership is read from the plan's ``coverage_matrix`` when
    supplied (a binding term -> its ``section_ids``); a section that owns a
    binding term is protected from being dropped as the LAST owner.

    Fail-open: ``None`` / empty contract -> an empty ledger (no locks, no binding
    owners), so ``update_outline`` validation is a no-op and the run is
    byte-identical.
    """
    ledger: dict[str, Any] = {
        "binding_term_owners": {},
        "locked_titles": [],
        "ordered_titles": [],
        "binding_term_ids": [],
    }
    if contract is None:
        return ledger

    # binding term ids: every HARD term across the contract.
    binding_ids: list[str] = []
    all_terms = getattr(contract, "all_terms", None)
    if callable(all_terms):
        try:
            for t in all_terms():
                if _is_hard(t):
                    tid = _norm(getattr(t, "term_id", ""))
                    if tid:
                        binding_ids.append(tid)
        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open
            binding_ids = []
    ledger["binding_term_ids"] = _dedup(binding_ids)

    # exact-title / order locks: explicit section requirements.
    locked: list[str] = []
    ordered: list[tuple[int, str]] = []
    sections = getattr(contract, "sections", None)
    if isinstance(sections, (list, tuple)):
        for sec in sections:
            title = _term_value_text(getattr(sec, "title", None))
            if not title:
                continue
            if getattr(sec, "exact_title_lock", False):
                locked.append(title)
            order = getattr(sec, "order", None)
            if isinstance(order, int) and getattr(sec, "exact_title_lock", False):
                ordered.append((order, title))
    ledger["locked_titles"] = _dedup(locked)
    ledger["ordered_titles"] = [t for _, t in sorted(ordered, key=lambda x: x[0])]

    # binding-term -> owning sections (from the coverage matrix). Only bindings
    # whose term id is in binding_ids get last-owner protection.
    owners: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
    matrix = coverage_matrix
    if matrix is None:
        matrix = getattr(getattr(contract, "plan", None), "coverage_matrix", None)
    if isinstance(matrix, (list, tuple)):
        binding_set = set(ledger["binding_term_ids"])
        # map requirement/section ids to section TITLES via the contract sections.
        sec_id_to_title = _section_id_to_title(contract)
        for binding in matrix:
            tid = _norm(getattr(binding, "contract_term_id", ""))
            if tid and tid not in binding_set:
                # the coverage-matrix may bind a coverage requirement whose
                # statement term is hard; keep only genuinely-binding ids.
                continue
            sec_ids = getattr(binding, "section_ids", None) or []
            titles = [
                sec_id_to_title.get(_norm(sid), _norm(sid))
                for sid in sec_ids if _norm(sid)
            ]
            titles = [t for t in titles if t]
            if tid and titles:
                owners.setdefault(tid, [])
                for t in titles:
                    if t not in owners[tid]:
                        owners[tid].append(t)
    ledger["binding_term_owners"] = owners
    return ledger


def _section_id_to_title(contract: Any) -> dict[str, str]:
    out: dict[str, str] = {}
    sections = getattr(contract, "sections", None)
    if isinstance(sections, (list, tuple)):
        for sec in sections:
            sid = _norm(getattr(sec, "section_id", ""))
            title = _term_value_text(getattr(sec, "title", None))
            if sid and title:
                out[sid] = title
    return out


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# FEED 3 — the gate's retrieval scope for the outline-stage gap search
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def gate_scope_for_gap_search(
    projection: Any, *, base_question: str = "",
) -> tuple[Any, Optional[dict[str, Any]]]:
    """The ``(research_frame, protocol)`` a :class:`RetrievalProjection` compiles.

    THE BUG FIX seam (both designers): ``outline_agent._tool_search_more_evidence``
    calls ``run_live_retrieval`` WITHOUT ``research_frame`` / ``protocol`` today, so
    the outline-stage gap search runs UNSCOPED even under a hard journal / language
    / jurisdiction contract. This returns the SAME scope levers the initial
    retrieval used, so the gap query keeps that scope.

    Fail-open: a ``None`` projection (or one that routes nothing) yields
    ``(None, None)`` — exactly the champion ``run_live_retrieval`` defaults, so the
    gap search is byte-identical to today.
    """
    if projection is None:
        return (None, None)
    frame = None
    protocol = None
    to_frame = getattr(projection, "to_research_frame", None)
    if callable(to_frame):
        try:
            frame = to_frame()
        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open
            frame = None
    to_proto = getattr(projection, "to_protocol", None)
    if callable(to_proto):
        try:
            protocol = to_proto(base_question=base_question)
        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open
            protocol = None
    return (frame, protocol)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# update_outline validation — reject dropping a lock / last binding owner
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def validate_revision_against_ledger(
    ledger: Optional[dict[str, Any]],
    *,
    titles_before: list[str],
    titles_after: list[str],
) -> list[str]:
    """Return a list of VIOLATION reasons if the revision drops an explicit lock or
    the last owner of a binding term. Empty list => the revision is allowed.

    ``titles_before`` / ``titles_after`` are the section title lists before and
    after the proposed op set. A violation is:

      * a ``locked_title`` present before but absent after (explicit title lock
        dropped), OR
      * a binding term whose EVERY owning section is absent after (the last owner
        of a binding requirement dropped).

    Fail-open: a ``None`` / empty ledger => ``[]`` (no constraint), byte-identical.
    A retitle that keeps the same count but renames a locked title IS a violation
    (the locked title disappeared) — the caller may then defer the op.
    """
    if not ledger:
        return []
    before = {_norm(t) for t in (titles_before or []) if _norm(t)}
    after = {_norm(t) for t in (titles_after or []) if _norm(t)}
    violations: list[str] = []

    for locked in (ledger.get("locked_titles") or []):
        locked_n = _norm(locked)
        if locked_n in before and locked_n not in after:
            violations.append(f"explicit_title_lock_dropped:{locked_n!r}")

    owners: dict[str, list[str]] = ledger.get("binding_term_owners") or {}
    for tid, sec_titles in owners.items():
        present_before = [t for t in sec_titles if _norm(t) in before]
        if not present_before:
            # the binding term had no owner present before — nothing to protect.
            continue
        present_after = [t for t in sec_titles if _norm(t) in after]
        if not present_after:
            violations.append(f"last_owner_of_binding_term_dropped:{tid!r}")

    return violations


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# small utils
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def _dedup(items: list[str]) -> list[str]:
    out: list[str] = []
    seen: set[str] = set()
    for it in items:
        it = _norm(it)
        if it and it.lower() not in seen:
            seen.add(it.lower())
            out.append(it)
    return out

    seeded plans and returns an updated result of the SAME shape the caller already expects.

    ``seed = supplied_seed or legacy_call_outline(...)``). The legacy path and every
    existing caller signature are preserved; the four ``gate_*`` / ``seed_outline``
